data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- self • 2026-02-23T15:40 • handoff

Check your hook and mail, then act on the hook if present:
1. `gt hook` - shows hooked work (if any)
2. `gt mail inbox` - check for messages
3. If work is hooked → execute it immediately
4. If nothing hooked → wait for instructions

content.content:
🪝 Hook Status: deacon/
Role: deacon

🚀 AUTONOMOUS MODE - Work on hook triggers immediate execution

🪝 Hooked: skogtown-wisp-qn9335: 🤝 HANDOFF: Routine patrol cycle 21
No molecule attached (hooked bead still triggers autonomous work)

Next: Attach a molecule to start work: gt mol attach <bead-id> <molecule-id>

content.thinking:
I have a handoff mail hooked. Per the instructions, if I find mail on my hook (not a patrol wisp), GUPP applies: read the mail content, interpret the prose instructions, and execute them. But this is a handoff message, so I should read it for situational awareness, then create a patrol wisp.

Let me read the handoff message first, then unhook it and create a new patrol.

content: IDLE_CHECK: No active work on hook. If idle, start patrol: gt deacon patrol

content.thinking: The `bd show` command is crashing with a nil pointer dereference in the Dolt storage layer. This is a known issue with wisp beads - they use a different storage path. Let me try reading the mail directly instead, and then proceed with creating a new patrol.

content.text: Dolt storage crash on wisp bead. Let me read the handoff mail directly and proceed.

○ skogtown-mol-lpn5 [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · OPEN]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-23 · Updated: 2026-02-23

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ○ skogtown-mol-7pk2: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-mol-d7xv: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-mol-j50o: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-mol-rpqx: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
